file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for HP ID (HPID) authentication operations including sign-in, sign-up, account verification, and country selection dismissal. Contains static methods for complete authentication flows used during user onboarding and login processes in the Instant Ink application.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for HP ID authentication operations. Contains methods for dismissing country selector popups, performing sign-in with email and password, completing full sign-in flows, creating new user accounts with sign-up, verifying account codes from email, and executing complete sign-up flows with verification. All methods are static and work with Playwright page objects to interact with HPID authentication pages.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where country selector may appear

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on HPID pages. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page object. Uses a try-except block to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the country selector is not present or the click fails for any reason, the exception is caught and passed silently, allowing the flow to continue. This is a defensive method used to handle optional UI elements that may or may not appear depending on user location or session state.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID sign-in page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the core HPID sign-in interaction by filling in username and password fields and submitting the form. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password entry step. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This method handles the two-step HPID login flow where username and password are entered on separate form steps.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID authentication flow

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for authentication

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete end-to-end HPID sign-in flow including dismissing country selector and clicking the sign-in option. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to handle any country selection popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the sign-in option button to appear using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000) with a 20 second timeout, then clicks it to navigate to the sign-in form. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to complete the username and password entry and form submission. This is the primary method to use for full sign-in flows in test scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID sign-up page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new user account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new user account

functions.what_it_does: Fills out and submits the HPID account creation form with user details. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with the hardcoded value 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email parameter using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password parameter using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form. This method handles the account creation form but does not handle email verification.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the verification code entry page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where the verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the email verification code and submits it to confirm the new HPID account. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the submit code button to be available using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Sets up retry logic with 3 attempts and 10 second wait intervals. In each retry attempt, waits for the specified time using page.wait_for_timeout(wait_time * 1000), then calls common.fetch_verification_code(email) to retrieve the verification code from the email service. If a verification code is successfully retrieved, fills the code input field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code), clicks the submit button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(), logs a success message with the email and environment details using framework_logger.info(), and breaks out of the retry loop. If the code fetch fails, logs a warning with the attempt number and exception using framework_logger.warning(). After all retries, if no verification code was obtained, logs an error using framework_logger.error() and returns early. Finally waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the complete sign-up flow

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant account to be created

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete end-to-end HPID account creation flow including country dismissal, navigation to sign-up form, account creation, and email verification. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to handle any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the create account button to appear using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000) with a 20 second timeout, then clicks it to navigate to the sign-up form. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill and submit the registration form using the provided email and the default password from common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to handle the email verification code entry and submission. This is the primary method for complete new user onboarding in test scenarios.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides a complete abstraction layer for HPID authentication operations. All methods are static, making them easy to call without instantiation. The module follows a pattern of providing both granular methods (sign_in, sign_up) and composite methods (complete_sign_in, complete_sign_up) that combine multiple steps. The confirm_account_code method includes robust retry logic for handling email verification code retrieval, which is critical for test reliability. The dismiss_select_country method uses defensive programming with try-except to handle optional UI elements gracefully. The module depends on HPIDPage for locators and common module for utilities like DEFAULT_PASSWORD and fetch_verification_code.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for landing page interactions, validations, and testing. Provides methods for accessing the landing page, verifying refer-a-friend banners, validating page sections, testing navigation links, verifying hyperlinks in footer, testing cookie settings, validating FAQ links, selecting countries, and validating emergency banners with different color schemes. Supports both desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) viewport testing.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for testing and validating the Instant Ink landing page. Contains methods for page navigation, privacy banner handling, refer-a-friend banner verification, section visibility validation, complete OOBE and MOBE flow testing, hyperlink validation, cookie settings testing, FAQ link validation, country selection, and emergency banner validation with multiple color schemes. Supports both desktop and mobile viewport testing with separate methods for each.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like LandingPageHelper.access(page)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to navigate to the landing page

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Instant Ink landing page URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() with the URL from common._instantink_url and a 120 second timeout. After navigation, calls page.wait_for_load_state('load') to ensure the page DOM is fully loaded before proceeding. This is a basic navigation method without any additional interactions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to access the landing page with privacy banner handling

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the landing page and handles privacy banner acceptance and optional close banner dismissal. First calls LandingPageHelper.open(page) to navigate to the landing page URL and wait for load. Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls privacy_banner_page.accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy consent if it appears. Creates a LandingPage instance. Uses a try-except block to attempt clicking the close banner button using landing_page.close_banner.click(). If the close banner is not present or the click fails, catches the exception and prints an error message. This is the recommended method for accessing the landing page in tests as it handles common popups.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance where the RaF banner should be verified

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Number of free months expected to be displayed in the RaF banner. Defaults to 1.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) promotional banner is visible and displays the correct number of free months. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RaF banner element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_banner). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30 second timeout. Retrieves the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is contained in the actual banner text, raising an assertion error with details if not found. Logs a success message with the expected text using framework_logger.info(). Returns True if verification passes.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance where the RaF footnote should be verified

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend footnote element is visible on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RaF footnote element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_footnote). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the footnote is visible with a 30 second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to evaluate viewport position

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check

functions.what_it_does: Checks whether a page section is currently visible within the browser viewport using JavaScript evaluation. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript code in the browser context. The JavaScript function takes the section_selector as a parameter, uses document.querySelector() to find the element, returns false if element is not found, gets the element's bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect(), and checks if the element's top is at or below 0 and bottom is at or below the window's inner height, indicating the element is fully within the viewport. Returns the boolean result from the JavaScript evaluation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing custom actions at each step. Called with step name, page object, and screenshot_only flag.

functions.what_it_does: Executes a comprehensive end-to-end test of the consumer landing page in desktop (OOBE - Out of Box Experience) viewport. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log errors. Navigates to the hardcoded stage1 landing page URL with 120 second timeout. Attempts to accept privacy banner by waiting for and clicking the OneTrust accept button with 10 second timeout, logging success or debug message if not found. Waits for sign up header button to be visible with 200 second timeout and clicks it. Attempts privacy banner acceptance again. Waits for HPID page to appear with 50 second timeout. Calls optional callback with 'signup_function' step name. Logs success message. Returns to landing page URL. Accepts privacy banner again. Waits for sign up button visibility. Tests How It Works section by clicking the tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, waiting for section visibility, verifying section is in viewport using verify_section_is_in_viewport(), logging result, and calling callback. Repeats similar pattern for Instant Paper Plan section, Plans section, and FAQs section. Each section test includes tab click, scroll wait, visibility check, viewport verification, logging, and optional callback. Catches any exception during the flow and logs error with exception details using framework_logger.error().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing custom actions at each step

functions.what_it_does: Executes a comprehensive end-to-end test of the consumer landing page in mobile (MOBE - Mobile Experience) viewport. Similar to consumer_landing_page_oobe but adapted for mobile navigation patterns. Wraps all operations in try-except block. Navigates to stage1 landing page URL. Accepts privacy banner. Waits for mobile menu button (hamburger menu) to be visible with 200 second timeout and clicks it. Waits for mobile sign up button to be visible and clicks it. Accepts privacy banner again. Waits for HPID page to appear. Calls optional callback. Logs success. Returns to landing page. Accepts privacy banner. Waits for mobile menu button and clicks it. Tests How It Works section by clicking mobile how it works tab, waiting for scroll, verifying section visibility and viewport position, logging result, and calling callback. For subsequent sections (Instant Paper, Plans, FAQs), opens mobile menu before each section test. Repeats pattern of clicking mobile menu, clicking section tab, waiting for scroll, verifying visibility and viewport, logging, and callback for each section. Uses mobile-specific locators like mobe_menu_button, mobe_sign_up_button, mobe_how_it_works_tab, mobe_instant_paper_tab, mobe_plans_tab, mobe_faqs_tab, and mobe_faqs_section. Catches exceptions and logs errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page in desktop viewport. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the never_worry_section element to be visible with 50 second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed() with 50 second timeout. Logs success message using framework_logger.info(). Calls optional callback with 'never_worry' step name and page object. Catches exceptions and logs error with exception details.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page in mobile viewport. Identical to validate_never_worry_section_oobe but uses the mobile-specific locator mobe_never_work_section. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the mobe_never_work_section element to be visible with 50 second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed. Logs success message. Calls optional callback. Catches exceptions and logs error.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport hyperlink testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots of opened pages

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page in desktop viewport by clicking each link, verifying the opened page URL, taking optional screenshots, and closing the new tabs. Wraps all operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. For HP.com link: waits for footer_hp_link to be visible, scrolls it into view, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new page when link is clicked, brings new page to front, waits for load state, uses expect() to verify URL matches regex pattern for hp.com home page with 60 second timeout, logs success, calls optional callback with 'hpcom' step name and new page, closes new page. Repeats similar pattern for Privacy Statement link (verifies privacy.html URL, waits 5 seconds for full load before screenshot), HP Connected Terms of Use link (verifies hpsmart.com/tou URL), Cookie Settings link (verifies use-of-cookies.html URL, waits 5 seconds), Your Privacy Choices link (verifies your-privacy-choices.html URL), and Instant Ink Terms of Service link (verifies instantink terms URL). Each link test includes visibility wait, new page capture, load wait, URL verification with regex, logging, optional callback, and new page closure. Catches exceptions and logs error with exception details.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport hyperlink testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page in mobile viewport. Identical to hyperlinks_validation but sets mobile viewport size on each opened page. For each link (HP.com, Privacy Statement, Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Privacy Choices, Instant Ink Terms), follows the same pattern: waits for link visibility, scrolls into view, captures new page on click, sets viewport size to 400x740 using new_page.set_viewport_size(), waits for load, verifies URL with regex, logs success, calls optional callback, closes new page. The viewport size setting ensures screenshots and validations are done in mobile dimensions. Uses the same footer link locators as desktop version. Catches exceptions and logs errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport cookie settings testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Tests the cookie settings functionality on the landing page in desktop viewport by opening the cookie settings page, clicking the cookie preference button, verifying the preference modal appears with correct title, taking optional screenshot, and closing the modal. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for footer_cookies link to be visible with 50 second timeout. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new page when footer_cookies is clicked. Waits for new page to load with 60 second timeout. Uses expect() to verify new page URL matches use-of-cookies.html regex pattern. Logs success. Scrolls the cookies_preference button into view on the new page using new_page.locator() with the element selector. Waits for cookies_preference button to be visible and clicks it. Logs success. Waits 5 seconds for cookie preference modal to appear using time.sleep(). Gets the text content of the cookies_preference_title element. Checks if the title text equals 'Privacy Preference Center', logging success if match or error if mismatch. Waits for cookies_preference_close button to be visible. Calls optional callback with 'cookie_preferences' step name and new page. Clicks the close button to dismiss the modal. Logs success. Closes the new page. Catches exceptions and logs error.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport cookie settings testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Tests the cookie settings functionality on the landing page in mobile viewport. Identical to cookies_settings_oobe but sets mobile viewport size on the opened page. Follows the same pattern: waits for footer_cookies link, captures new page on click, sets viewport to 400x740 using new_page.set_viewport_size(), waits for load, verifies URL, logs success, scrolls cookie preference button into view, clicks it, logs success, waits 5 seconds, gets and verifies preference title text, waits for close button, calls optional callback, clicks close button, logs success, closes new page. The viewport size setting ensures the cookie preference modal is tested in mobile dimensions. Catches exceptions and logs errors. Note: The code has a syntax error at the end with an orphaned expect statement that appears to be leftover code.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for FAQ link validation

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensively validates all FAQ section links on the landing page by iterating through FAQ sections, expanding questions, clicking links within answers, and verifying the opened pages. Stores the original page URL. Creates a LandingPage instance. Scrolls the FAQs section into view and verifies it is visible. Defines a list of FAQ sections with tuples containing section name and section ID: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans. For each FAQ section, logs the section name being validated. If the current URL differs from original (due to navigation), navigates back to original URL and waits for networkidle. Locates the section tab using data-testid accordion and aria-controls attribute matching the section ID, clicks it, and waits 500ms. Checks if a 'Show more' button exists in the section, clicks it if present, and waits 500ms. Locates all question buttons in the section using aria-controls attribute pattern. Gets the count of questions. For each question index, re-locates the questions (to handle DOM updates), breaks if index exceeds count, gets the nth question, clicks it, waits 300ms, gets the aria-controls ID from the question, skips if no ID, locates the answer element by ID, verifies answer is visible with 3 second timeout, locates all links within the answer, gets link count. For each link index, re-locates answer and links (to handle DOM updates), breaks if index exceeds count, gets the nth link, gets the href attribute, skips if href is null or starts with mailto: or tel:. Clicks the link, waits 2 seconds, gets all pages in the context, gets the last page (newly opened), brings it to front, waits for networkidle with 35 second timeout. Uses a nested try-except to check if the page contains the HP branding image using data-testid, logs success if found, closes the page. If branding not found, expects the URL to contain '/terms' with 30 second timeout, logs success, closes the page, navigates back to original URL, waits for domcontentloaded, clicks the section tab again, waits 300ms, clicks show more if present, waits 300ms, re-locates questions, clicks the question at index i if it exists, waits 300ms. Catches link click exceptions, logs warning, and continues. After processing all links in a question, attempts to click the question again to collapse it, using try-except to handle failures. Catches section-level exceptions, logs warning with section name and exception, and continues to next section. After all sections, if current URL differs from original, navigates back to original URL and waits for domcontentloaded.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for country selection

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to select. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country from the country selector on the landing page by clicking the flag icon and then clicking the appropriate locale option. Creates a LandingPage instance. Clicks the flag_icon to open the country selector dropdown. Calls load_locale_data() to get the locale mapping data. Retrieves the languages array for the specified country from the locale map. Gets the country code and converts it to lowercase. Selects the first language from the languages array. Constructs the locale code string in format 'language-countrycode'. Locates the country selector option using data-testid attribute with the constructed locale code and clicks it to select the country.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for emergency banner validation

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to validate banner for. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the emergency banner on the landing page displays the correct background color and icon for the specified country. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the parent element of the emergency banner using landing_page.emergency_banner.locator('..'). Calls common.get_font_details() to extract CSS properties including background color from the banner element. Gets the detected background color from the returned data. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected RGB color value, and expected SVG path for the icon (or None for countries without SVG path). For Finland: Red Minus Banner with rgb(190, 19, 19) and minus icon path. For Bulgaria: Orange Alert Banner with rgb(208, 103, 2) and no SVG path. For Portugal: Green Done Banner with rgb(28, 122, 23) and checkmark icon path. For Australia: Blue Done Banner with rgb(2, 122, 174) and checkmark icon path. For Norway: White Done Banner with rgb(255, 255, 255) and checkmark icon path. For Poland: Grey Done Banner with rgb(64, 64, 64) and checkmark icon path. For Estonia: Black Done Banner with rgb(33, 33, 33) and checkmark icon path. Gets the configuration for the target country from the dictionary. Logs the banner name being tested. Asserts that the detected color matches the expected color from config, raising assertion error with details if mismatch. Logs color validation success. If the config has an expected_icon_path (not None), locates the SVG path element within the emergency banner, gets its 'd' attribute value, asserts it matches the expected path, and logs SVG path validation success. If expected_icon_path is None (Bulgaria case), locates the element with class 'orangeBannerIcon', asserts its count is greater than 0, and logs orange banner icon found. Logs overall validation success with banner name.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extremely comprehensive helper module for landing page testing with 14 static methods covering a wide range of test scenarios. The module demonstrates excellent test design patterns including separate methods for desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) viewports, optional callback functions for screenshot capture, robust error handling with try-except blocks, detailed logging at each step, and thorough validation of UI elements, URLs, and content. The validate_faq_links method is particularly complex with nested loops and DOM re-querying to handle dynamic content. The validate_new_emergency_banner method uses a data-driven approach with country configurations. The module has extensive dependencies on page objects and common utilities. Note: There is a syntax error at the end of cookies_settings_mobe with an orphaned expect statement that should be removed.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for interacting with the OSS (Online Service Simulator) emulator used for testing printer onboarding and enrollment flows. Contains methods for accessing the emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, and handling various onboarding scenarios including OOBE and POOBE (Post Out of Box Experience) flows.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the OSS emulator web interface. Contains methods for navigating to the emulator, signing in with HPID, filling printer configuration details including serial number, SKU, UUID, cloud ID, and fingerprint, selecting app types, starting emulator flows, setting up complete OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting terms and services, declining HP Plus offers, continuing through dynamic security notices and value propositions, selecting printer replacement options, activating HP Plus, selecting countries, verifying reminder checkboxes and trial text, and accessing learn more pages.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to navigate to OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the OSS emulator URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() with the URL from common._oss_url. Waits for the page load state to complete using page.wait_for_load_state('load') with a 60 second timeout to ensure the emulator interface is ready for interaction.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for OSS emulator sign-in

functions.what_it_does: Signs into the OSS emulator by clicking the dev tab and then clicking the HPID login button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dev_tab element to navigate to the developer options section using oss_emulator_page.dev_tab.click(). Then clicks the oss_hpid_login button to initiate the HPID authentication flow using oss_emulator_page.oss_hpid_login.click().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for clicking 3M button

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the Yeti 3M button in the OSS emulator after a 5 second wait. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) to allow the page to stabilize. Then clicks the yeti_3m button with a 60 second timeout using oss_emulator_page.yeti_3m.click(timeout=60000). The 3M button likely refers to a specific printer model or configuration option in the emulator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for filling emulator form

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, and fingerprint attributes

functions.what_it_does: Fills all printer configuration fields in the OSS emulator form with data from the printer object. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Fills the serial_number field with printer.entity_id using oss_emulator_page.serial_number.fill(). Fills the sku field with printer.model_number. Fills the uuid field with printer.device_uuid. Fills the cloud_id field with the base64-decoded value of printer.postcard using base64.b64decode(printer.postcard).decode('utf-8'). Fills the first fingerprint field with the base64-decoded value of printer.fingerprint using oss_emulator_page.fingerprint.first.fill() and base64.b64decode(printer.fingerprint).decode('utf-8'). Selects 'completed' option for the language_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.language_config.select_option('completed'). Selects 'completed' option for the country_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.country_config.select_option('completed'). This method configures all necessary printer identification and configuration parameters for the emulator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for app type selection

parameters.name: app_type
parameters.description: App type to select. If None, uses common._simulator_platform or defaults to 'Gotham'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects the application type in the OSS emulator. Determines the app type to use by first checking if app_type parameter is provided, otherwise using common._simulator_platform, or defaulting to 'Gotham' if neither is set. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_tab to navigate to the app configuration section using oss_emulator_page.app_tab.click(). Selects the determined app type from the app_type dropdown using oss_emulator_page.app_type.select_option(app_type). The app type likely determines which application platform (e.g., Gotham, HP Smart) the emulator will simulate.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for starting emulator flow

functions.what_it_does: Starts the OSS emulator flow by clicking the start flow button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the start_flow_button element using oss_emulator_page.start_flow_button.click() to initiate the emulator simulation with the configured printer and app settings.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for complete emulator setup

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object with configuration data

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete OSS emulator setup flow by calling multiple helper methods in sequence. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the printer model. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill all printer configuration fields. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app platform. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the emulator flow. This is the primary method for setting up the emulator for standard OOBE testing.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for POOBE flow setup

functions.what_it_does: Configures the OSS emulator for Post Out of Box Experience (POOBE) flow by clicking specific checkboxes. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_post_oobe_agreements_features element to access POOBE configuration options using oss_emulator_page.app_post_oobe_agreements_features.click(). Clicks the subscription_checkbox to enable subscription-related features using oss_emulator_page.subscription_checkbox.click(). Clicks the oobe_checkbox to enable OOBE-related features using oss_emulator_page.oobe_checkbox.click(). This configures the emulator to simulate a post-setup enrollment scenario.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for complete POOBE emulator setup

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object with configuration data

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete OSS emulator setup flow for POOBE (Post Out of Box Experience) testing. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the printer model. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill all printer configuration fields. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app platform. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.setup_poobe_flow(page) to configure POOBE-specific options. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the emulator flow. This is the primary method for setting up the emulator for POOBE testing scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for accepting connected printing services

functions.what_it_does: Accepts the connected printing services terms by clicking the accept all button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load') with a 120 second timeout. Then clicks the accept_all_button element using oss_emulator_page.accept_all_button.click() to accept all connected printing services terms and conditions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for declining HP Plus

functions.what_it_does: Declines the HP Plus offer during the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the last occurrence of the do_not_activate_hp_plus element with a 120 second timeout using oss_emulator_page.do_not_activate_hp_plus.last.click(timeout=120000). Then clicks the decline_hp_plus button to confirm the decline action using oss_emulator_page.decline_hp_plus.click(). This method handles the HP Plus opt-out flow during printer setup.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for continuing past dynamic security notice

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the dynamic security notice screen by clicking the continue button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the ds_continue_button element with a 200 second timeout using oss_emulator_page.ds_continue_button.click(timeout=200000). The dynamic security notice informs users about printer security features and cartridge authentication.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for continuing past value proposition

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the value proposition screen by clicking the next button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the next_button element with a 120 second timeout using oss_emulator_page.next_button.click(timeout=120000). The value proposition screen typically presents benefits and features of the service to the user.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for selecting replace printer option

functions.what_it_does: Selects the replace printer option and continues to the next step. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the second occurrence (index 1) of the replace_printer_radio_button using oss_emulator_page.replace_printer_radio_button.nth(1).click(). Then clicks the continue_button to proceed using oss_emulator_page.continue_button.click(). This method handles the scenario where a user is replacing an existing printer with a new one.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for activating HP Plus

functions.what_it_does: Activates HP Plus by clicking the continue button and then the activate button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the Hp_Plus_Continue_button to proceed to the activation screen using oss_emulator_page.Hp_Plus_Continue_button.click(). Then clicks the Activate_HP_Plus button to confirm activation using oss_emulator_page.Activate_HP_Plus.click(). This method handles the HP Plus opt-in flow during printer setup.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for country selection

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country from the country selector based on the GlobalState country code. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Locates the country element using the ID attribute matching GlobalState.country_code with page.locator(). Wraps the selection logic in a try-except block. Scrolls the country element into view if needed using select_country.scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Checks if the country element is visible with a 100 second timeout using select_country.is_visible(timeout=100000). If visible, logs the country code using framework_logger.info(), clicks the country element, and clicks the continue_selection button using oss_emulator_page.continue_selection.click(). Waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10) after selection. If any exception occurs during the selection process, passes silently to allow the flow to continue.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for verification

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Number of trial months expected to be displayed

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Instant Ink reminder checkbox is visible, checked, and displays the correct trial text with the expected number of months. Locates the reminder checkbox using data-testid 'instant-ink-reminder'. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the checkbox is visible with a 500 second timeout. Verifies the checkbox is checked with a 2 second timeout using expect().to_be_checked(). Locates the reminder text element using class selector starting with 'styles__CheckBoxText'. Uses expect().to_have_text() with a regex pattern to verify the text contains 'Remind me 2 weeks before my {expected_months}-month trial of HP Instant Ink expires.' with non-breaking spaces. Locates the trial info disclaimer using data-testid 'general-monthly-fee-disclaimer'. Verifies the disclaimer is visible. Verifies the disclaimer text equals 'After the trial, a monthly fee will be automatically charged unless canceled.' using expect().to_have_text().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for accessing learn more

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the learn more button to access additional information. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the learn_more_button element with a 120 second timeout using oss_emulator_page.learn_more_button.click(timeout=120000). This typically opens a page or modal with more details about the service or feature.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides comprehensive utilities for OSS emulator interaction with 17 static methods covering all aspects of emulator setup and flow navigation. The module demonstrates good separation of concerns with granular methods for individual actions (access, sign_in, fill, etc.) and composite methods for complete flows (setup_oss_emulator, setup_oss_emulator_poobe_flow). The fill_oss_emulator method handles base64 decoding of printer credentials. The module supports both standard OOBE and POOBE testing scenarios. Several methods include defensive programming with try-except blocks and configurable timeouts. The verify_reminder_checkbox_and_trial_text method uses Playwright's expect assertions for robust verification. The module depends on OssEmulatorPage for locators, common module for configuration, and GlobalState for environment settings.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for overview page operations including cartridge verification, printer grouping validation, plan suggestion banner verification, plan upgrade information validation, unsubscribed status card verification, and invoice download functionality. Used for testing the main overview/dashboard page of the Instant Ink application.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with and validating the overview page. Contains methods for verifying installed cartridge messages for different cartridge types, validating printer grouping by status, verifying plan suggestion banners for upgrading or downgrading, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status card elements, testing HP shop ink link navigation, testing enroll printer again functionality, and downloading past invoices with PDF verification.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, 'k,c')

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for cartridge verification

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k,c,m,y' or 'cmy')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the specified cartridge types are displayed as installed on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get individual cartridge types. Defines a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes to their selector strings: 'k' maps to kCartridgeItem selector, 'c' to cCartridgeItem, 'm' to mCartridgeItem, 'y' to yCartridgeItem, and 'cmy' to cmyCartridgeItem. For each cartridge type in the list, strips whitespace, checks if the type exists in the dictionary (raising RuntimeError if invalid), waits for the cartridge element to appear using page.wait_for_selector() with the selector from overview_page.get_selector() and 20 second timeout, and asserts the element is visible with an error message if not. This method validates that the correct cartridge status indicators are shown on the overview page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for verification

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Primary cartridge type string to verify

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Fallback cartridge type string to verify if primary fails

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to verify cartridge installation with a fallback option. Wraps the verification in a try-except block. First attempts to call OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge) with the primary cartridge parameter. If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the primary cartridge type is invalid or not found), catches the exception and calls OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2) with the fallback cartridge2 parameter. This method is useful when the exact cartridge configuration is uncertain and multiple valid configurations exist.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for printer section verification

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group name or status text to identify the printer section (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive')

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Expected number of printers: 'only one' or 'more than one'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the number of printers displayed in a specific status group section on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets all printer status section elements using overview_page.printers_grouped_by_status.all(). Initializes printers_grouped to None. Iterates through each section element, checking if the group parameter text is contained in the element's inner text. If found, assigns the element to printers_grouped and breaks the loop. Asserts that printers_grouped is not None and is visible, raising an error if the section is not found. Asserts that the group text is contained in the section's inner text. Locates all printer selector option elements within the section using overview_page.printer_selector_option and gets them as an array. If number parameter is 'only one', asserts the array length equals 1. If number parameter is 'more than one', asserts the array length is greater than 1. If number is neither, raises an Exception. This method validates printer grouping and counting on the overview page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for banner verification

parameters.name: banner_type
parameters.description: Type of banner to verify: 'downgrading' or 'upgrading'. Defaults to 'downgrading'.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a plan suggestion banner is visible and contains appropriate text for the specified banner type. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets the plan_suggestion_banner selector using overview_page.get_selector(). Locates the banner element using page.locator(). Waits for the banner to be visible with 30 second timeout using wait_for(). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible. Gets the banner text content and converts it to lowercase. If banner_type is 'downgrading', defines a list of downgrade keywords including 'downgrading', 'lower plan', 'reduce', 'save money', 'smaller plan', and asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text, raising an assertion error with the actual banner content if not found. If banner_type is 'upgrading', defines a list of upgrade keywords including 'upgrading', 'higher plan', 'more pages', 'increase', 'larger plan', and asserts at least one is present. If banner_type is neither, raises a ValueError. Prints a success message with the banner type. Returns the banner locator object.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for plan information verification

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: Expected number of pages in the upgraded plan as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the overview page displays the correct upgraded plan page count. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the plan_information element is visible with 120 second timeout. Gets the inner text of the plan_information element, removes periods and commas using replace(). Uses regex to find all digit sequences in the text with re.findall(). Extracts the second number from the list (index 1) which represents the plan pages. Asserts that the extracted plan pages value as a string equals the provided plan parameter, validating that the plan upgrade information is correctly displayed.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for unsubscribed status card verification

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that all expected elements are visible on the unsubscribed status card. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button is visible with 150 second timeout. Verifies the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with 30 second timeout. Verifies the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices element is visible with 30 second timeout. This method ensures all action buttons and links are present on the status card shown to unsubscribed users.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for HP shop ink link testing

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the HP shop ink button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies it opens the correct HP shop page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with 30 second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new page that opens when the button is clicked. Clicks the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button. Gets the new tab from the page info. Uses expect() to verify the new tab URL matches the regex pattern containing '/shop/pdp' with 30 second timeout, confirming navigation to the HP shop product page. Closes the new tab.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for enroll printer again testing

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the enroll printer again button and verifies the enrollment flow opens correctly. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Clicks the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new enrollment page that opens. Gets the enrollment page from the page info and brings it to front. Wraps the automatic printer updates handling in a try-except block. Attempts to call EnrollmentHelper.accept_automatic_printer_updates(enrollment_page) and logs success if the page appears. If an exception occurs, logs that the automatic printer updates page was not found or already passed. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance with the enrollment page. Uses expect() to verify the v3_content_area element is visible with 120 second timeout, confirming the enrollment confirmation page loaded. Closes the enrollment page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for invoice download testing

parameters.name: invoices_directory
parameters.description: Directory path where invoice PDF should be saved. Defaults to '/tmp/downloads'.

functions.what_it_does: Downloads past invoices from the unsubscribed status card and verifies the PDF file is created. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices element is visible with 30 second timeout. Creates the invoices directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the PDF page that opens when the download link is clicked. Clicks the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices element. Gets the PDF page from the page info and brings it to front. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(). Constructs the PDF file path by joining the invoices directory with 'invoice.pdf' filename. Saves the PDF using pdf_page.pdf(path=pdf_file). Closes the PDF page. Waits 2 seconds. Asserts that the PDF file exists at the specified path using os.path.exists(), raising an assertion error if not found. Takes a screenshot of the main page and saves it to the invoices directory as 'invoice_pdf_file_screenshot.png'. Logs success message with the PDF file path using framework_logger.info().

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides 8 static methods for comprehensive overview page testing. The module demonstrates good test design with methods covering cartridge verification, printer grouping validation, plan suggestion banners, unsubscribed user flows, and file download verification. The verify_cartridge_installed_message method uses a dictionary mapping approach for flexible cartridge type validation. The verify_plan_suggestion_banner method uses keyword matching for flexible text validation. The download_past_invoices_on_status_card method includes file system operations and PDF generation verification. The module has dependencies on OverviewPage, EnrollmentHelper, ConfirmationPage, and framework utilities. Note: The import statement for 're' appears twice at the beginning of the file, which is redundant but not harmful.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations including billing cycle selection, billing table data verification, currency value normalization and validation, complete billing section verification with multiple data points, FAQ validation with link testing, no-pens print history validation, invoice download functionality, notification verification, and plan information validation. Provides robust utilities for testing print and payment history features.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with and validating the print history page. Contains methods for selecting billing cycle periods, verifying billing table data including pages printed and charges, normalizing and validating currency values across different formats and locales, verifying complete billing sections with multiple data points, validating all billing cycles data from a list, testing FAQ links and navigation, validating no-pens print history page state, downloading all invoices, verifying notifications in print history table, and validating plan information and current total values.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like PrintHistoryHelper.verify_billing_table_data(page, 150)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing cycle verification

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Clicks the select_1_option to select the first previous billing cycle. Uses Playwright's expect() with not_to_contain_text() to verify the billing_cycle_period_title does not contain the text 'Current', confirming a previous period was selected.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing cycle selection

parameters.name: period_index
parameters.description: Index of the billing period to select: 1, 2, or 3

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period from the dropdown and waits for the table to update. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs the action using framework_logger.info(). Uses conditional logic to select the appropriate period option: if period_index is 1, clicks select_1_option; if 2, clicks select_2_option; if 3, clicks select_3_option; otherwise raises ValueError with invalid index message. Logs the selected period index. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with 10 second timeout, ensuring the table has updated. Logs that the billing table updated after period selection.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing data verification

parameters.name: total_pages_printed
parameters.description: Expected total number of pages printed to verify

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the total pages printed value in the billing table matches the expected value. Logs the start of validation using framework_logger.info(). Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Gets the inner text of the total_pages_printed element. Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the text. Checks if the numbers list is empty, raising ValueError if no numbers were found in the text. Asserts that the first number in the list equals the expected total_pages_printed parameter, raising an assertion error with details if mismatch.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for text extraction

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Either a locator object or a string selector

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Extracts text content from a page element, handling both locator objects and string selectors. Checks if the selector has an 'inner_text' attribute using hasattr(). If it does (meaning it's already a locator object), calls selector.inner_text() directly and returns the result. If it doesn't (meaning it's a string selector), uses page.locator() to create a locator, gets the first element using .first, calls inner_text() on it, and returns the result. This utility method provides flexible text extraction regardless of selector type.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize (can be string, number, or None)

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values to a consistent format for comparison by removing currency symbols, handling different decimal separators, and converting special characters. Returns '0.00' if value is None or empty. Converts value to string, strips whitespace, replaces commas with periods (for European format), removes dollar signs, euro symbols, and pound symbols. Replaces non-breaking spaces and regular spaces. Checks if the normalized value is one of the empty/null indicators: '-', '', '—', 'N/A', 'n/a', returning '0.00' if so. Wraps the float conversion in a try-except block. Attempts to convert the normalized string to a float, then formats it as a string with 2 decimal places using f-string formatting. If conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This method ensures currency values from different locales and formats can be compared consistently.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for currency field validation

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Locator object or string selector for the currency field

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value (can be string, int, or float)

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field contains the expected value, with support for partial or exact matching. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector() to extract the actual text from the element. Checks if expected is an integer, and if so, divides it by 100 to convert from cents to currency units. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value() to normalize the expected value. If partial_match is True, replaces commas with periods in the actual text and asserts that the normalized expected value is contained in the actual text, raising an assertion error with details if not found. If partial_match is False, calls normalize_currency_value() on the actual text and asserts exact equality with the normalized expected value, raising an assertion error with both normalized and raw values if mismatch.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for billing section verification

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected values for various billing fields

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensively verifies all billing section data points against expected values from a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if 'total_pages_printed' key exists in expected_values, and if so, extracts the first number from the total_pages_printed element text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(), asserts it equals the expected value. Checks if 'pages' key exists, extracts the first number from the plan_pages_text element using get_text_from_selector() and extract_numbers_from_text(), asserts equality. Checks if 'rollover' key exists and its value is not '-', extracts the first number from rollover_pages_text, asserts equality. Checks if 'additional' key exists and its value is not '-', extracts the first number from additional_pages_text, asserts equality. Checks if 'plan_price' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with plan_price_text selector, expected value, and partial_match=True. Checks if 'overage_price' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with overage_description_text selector and partial_match=True. Checks if 'previous' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with previous_billing_text selector for exact match. Checks if 'charges' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with current_billing_text selector for exact match. Checks if 'tax' key exists and value is not '-', wraps in try-except to call validate_currency_field() with tax-amount data-testid selector, logs warning if element not found. Checks if 'total' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with current_total_text selector for exact match. This method provides comprehensive validation of all billing data points in a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for multi-cycle verification

parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing 'billing_cycle' key and expected values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Iterates through multiple billing cycles and verifies the data for each cycle against expected values. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Iterates through each row dictionary in the expected_data list. Converts the 'billing_cycle' value to 0-based index by subtracting 1. Logs the billing cycle being validated using framework_logger.info(). Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs the action. Locates all billing cycle options using page.locator() with the billing_cycle_options selector. Clicks the nth option corresponding to the billing_cycle_index using nth() and click(). Logs the selected option number. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout() to allow the table to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section() with the page and the current row dictionary to verify all data points for that cycle. This method enables data-driven testing of multiple billing cycles from a single test data structure.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for FAQ validation

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ questions and their associated links on the print and payment history page. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Clicks faq_question_1 to expand it. Uses expect() to verify faq_answer_1 is visible with 30 second timeout. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new page when faq_terms_of_service_link is clicked. Verifies the popup URL matches '/terms' regex pattern with 30 second timeout. Closes the popup. Clicks faq_question_2 to expand it. Verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks faq_overview_link. Verifies the overview page status_card_title is visible with 30 second timeout, confirming navigation to overview page. Clicks print_history_menu_link in the side menu to return. Clicks faq_question_2 again to expand it. Verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks the first update_plan_link. Verifies the update plan page page_title is visible, confirming navigation to update plan page. Clicks print_history_menu_link to return. Clicks faq_question_3 to expand it. Verifies faq_answer_3 is visible. Clicks faq_question_4 to expand it. Verifies faq_answer_4 is visible. Clicks the last update_plan_link. Verifies the update plan page page_title is visible. Clicks print_history_menu_link to return. This method comprehensively tests all FAQ interactions and link navigations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for no-pens validation

functions.what_it_does: Validates the print history page state when no pens (printers) are enrolled by checking which elements are visible and which are not. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_section is visible with 30 second timeout. Verifies page_title is visible. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify print_history_card_title is NOT visible with 30 second timeout. Verifies how_is_calculated_link is NOT visible. Verifies total_printed_pages is NOT visible. Verifies plan_pages_bar is NOT visible. Verifies plan_details_card is NOT visible. This method ensures the page displays correctly when there are no enrolled printers, showing only basic structure without billing details.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for invoice download

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the download all invoices button with retry logic and verifies the invoices page opens. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. In each attempt, wraps operations in try-except block. Clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify download_all_invoices_button is visible with 5 second timeout. If successful, breaks out of the loop. If exception occurs and it's the 59th attempt (last retry), raises the exception. Otherwise, logs the retry attempt number using framework_logger.info(), waits 60 seconds using time.sleep(), reloads the page using page.reload(), and uses expect() to verify print_history_card_title is visible with 90 second timeout after reload. After successful visibility, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when download_all_invoices_button is clicked. Gets the new tab from the page info. Asserts that 'invoices' is contained in the new tab URL, raising an assertion error with the actual URL if not found. This method handles the case where invoice generation may take time and requires page refresh.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for notification verification

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using is_hidden(). If hidden, clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table is visible with 30 second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep() to allow content to load. Gets the inner text of the entire print_history_table. Asserts that the notifications_text parameter is contained in the table content, raising an assertion error with both expected and actual content if not found.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for plan info verification

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value as a string

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan pages value as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information displays the correct price and page count. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with 90 second timeout. Gets the text content of the plan_price_text element. Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numbers from the plan info text. Asserts that the first number (index 0) equals the plan_value parameter, raising an assertion error with details if mismatch. Asserts that the second number (index 1) equals the plan_pages parameter, raising an assertion error with details if mismatch.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for current total verification

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected current total value as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total value in the billing section matches the expected value. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with 90 second timeout. Gets the text content of the first current_total_text element using .first.text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numbers from the current total text. Asserts that the first number (index 0) equals the total_value parameter, raising an assertion error with details if mismatch.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the most comprehensive helper module in the batch with 12 static methods covering all aspects of print history page testing. The module demonstrates excellent design patterns including utility methods for text extraction and currency normalization, data-driven testing support with verify_all_billing_cycles_data, robust retry logic in click_download_all_invoices, and comprehensive FAQ validation. The normalize_currency_value and validate_currency_field methods provide flexible currency handling across different locales and formats. The verify_correct_billing_section method uses a dictionary-based approach for flexible validation of multiple data points. The module has extensive dependencies on PrintHistoryPage, DashboardSideMenuPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and common utilities. The click_download_all_invoices method includes sophisticated retry logic with 60 attempts and 60-second intervals to handle invoice generation delays.
